concepts.lesson: An element is a substance that consists of only one type of atom. It has a fixed number of protons in its atomic nucleus, which determines the element's identity. Elements are the building blocks of matter and can be found naturally or artificially created.
key_points: Elements have unique properties based on their atomic structure
Elements can be naturally occurring or synthetically produced

concepts.lesson: A compound is a substance formed when two or more elements chemically bond together. Compounds have properties that are different from those of their constituent elements. The ratio of atoms in a compound is fixed and can be expressed by a chemical formula.
key_points: Compounds are made up of multiple elements
Compounds have unique properties based on the arrangement of atoms

concepts.lesson: A mixture is a physical combination of two or more substances where each substance retains its chemical identity. Mixtures can be homogeneous (uniform composition) or heterogeneous (non-uniform composition). The components of a mixture can be separated by physical means.
key_points: Mixtures are combinations of multiple substances
Components of mixtures retain their chemical identity

concepts.lesson: Atomic mass is the total number of protons and neutrons in an atom's nucleus. It is a measure of an element's atomic weight and is used to distinguish between different isotopes of an element.
key_points: Atomic mass is a measure of an element's atomic weight
Different isotopes have the same number of protons but differ in neutron count

concepts.lesson: The atomic number is the number of protons present in an atom's nucleus. It determines the element's identity and is unique to each element.
key_points: Atomic number determines an element's identity
Elements with different atomic numbers are chemically distinct
